rule_schema.standard_reference: Exact section or rule reference in standards/stack.md.
rule_schema.section: Human section name from the standard.
rule_schema.title: Short operational title.
rule_schema.requirement: Normative requirement extracted from the standard.
rule_schema.applies_to: Repo surfaces where the rule applies.
rule_schema.trigger_kinds: Change categories that should pull this rule into an applicable checklist.
rule_schema.phases: Workflow phases where this rule is relevant.
rule_schema.enforcement_mode: validator-required, checklist-required, checklist-and-validator, advisory, or backlog.
rule_schema.evidence_required: What an implementation or verification receipt should record when applicable.
rule_schema.automation: Current or intended mechanical enforcement owner.

rules.title: Use React Vite and React Router by default
rules.requirement: Use React + Vite + React Router for most applications.
evidence_required: New default apps use the React + Vite + React Router path unless a recorded stack exception applies.
The active plan records any deviation from the default web/PWA stack.

rules.title: Add Capacitor only for mobile packaging or native APIs
rules.requirement: Add Capacitor when Android/iOS packaging or native APIs are needed.
evidence_required: The plan or receipt records the Android/iOS packaging or native API need that justifies Capacitor.
Capacitor dependency and native wrapper work remain scoped to the app that needs them.

rules.title: Use TypeScript as the app language
evidence_required: App and template source use TypeScript unless an app-specific stack exception is recorded.
TypeScript verification remains part of the app check sequence.

rules.title: Use the standard UI styling and component stack
rules.requirement: Use Tailwind CSS, CSS variables, shadcn/ui, Radix primitives, and lucide-react for styling, design tokens, editable component source, accessible component behavior, and icons.
evidence_required: UI implementation uses the standard styling, token, component, primitive, and icon stack where applicable.
Any replacement UI kit, icon system, or styling framework is recorded as a stack exception.

rules.title: Use the standard validation form state and data stack
rules.requirement: Use Zod for runtime validation and schema inference, React Hook Form for forms, TanStack Query for server/cache state, and Zustand for small local state stores.
evidence_required: Form, validation, server-state, and small client-state needs use the standard libraries where applicable.
Any alternate validation, form, cache, or state library is justified in the project AGENTS.md or active plan.

rules.title: Use standard table and chart libraries
rules.requirement: Use TanStack Table for data tables and Recharts or ECharts for charts.
evidence_required: Complex table work uses TanStack Table where applicable.
Chart work uses Recharts or ECharts unless an alternate library is justified.

rules.title: Use Supabase as the default backend
rules.requirement: Use Supabase as the default backend, auth, storage, and Postgres provider.
evidence_required: Backend, auth, storage, and database choices use Supabase by default.
Any alternate backend provider is recorded as a stack exception with the reason.

rules.title: Use the standard verification libraries
rules.requirement: Use Vitest for unit tests, Testing Library for component tests, and Playwright for end-to-end and responsive UI checks.
evidence_required: Unit, component, e2e, and responsive UI checks use the standard verification libraries where applicable.
Any alternate test runner or browser verification tool is recorded as a stack exception.

rules.title: Use Next.js only for server-rendered or content-heavy needs
rules.requirement: Use Next.js when SEO or public content matters, SSR/server rendering is needed, route-level server functions reduce backend complexity, or the app is content-heavy or ecommerce-like.
evidence_required: Next.js adoption records the SEO, SSR, server-function, content-heavy, or ecommerce driver.
Next.js is not chosen for default web/PWA work without one of the recorded drivers.

rules.title: Use Expo only for native-first mobile apps
rules.requirement: Use Expo when native mobile UX is central, native device capabilities dominate, or web is secondary.
evidence_required: Expo adoption records the native-first mobile UX or device-capability reason.
Expo is not chosen when the app is primarily web/PWA with only packaging needs.

rules.title: Use Tauri only for desktop packaging or native desktop capabilities
rules.requirement: Use Tauri when desktop packaging is required or native desktop capabilities such as filesystem, tray, updater, or OS shortcuts are necessary.
evidence_required: Tauri adoption records the desktop packaging or native desktop capability need.
Desktop-only dependencies remain scoped to the app requiring desktop packaging.

rules.title: Install dependencies where they are used
rules.requirement: Install dependencies in the package where they are used.
evidence_required: New dependencies are added to the app or package that uses them.
App dependencies are not installed at the workspace root unless they are root tooling.

rules.title: Keep root dependencies limited to workspace tooling
rules.requirement: Keep root dependencies limited to workspace tooling.
evidence_required: Root dependencies are limited to tooling used by the workspace itself.
App runtime dependencies are scoped to app packages.

rules.title: Commit lockfiles for reproducibility
evidence_required: Dependency changes include matching lockfile updates.
Lockfiles are validated by the app verification or CI path.
